markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 release date gives traders roughly five months for a wide range of events to resolve ahead of launch. Recent delays from the original 2025 target and a brief May 2026 window have extended the runway, increasing the window for music drops, geopolitical developments, and pop culture milestones to occur first. High-volume outcomes such as new albums from major artists and potential ceasefires reflect ongoing trader focus on verifiable releases and news cycles that could conclude before Rockstar ships. Upcoming earnings calls from Take-Two and broader industry scheduling shifts around the title's dominance continue to shape expectations for what qualifies as "before" the highly anticipated open-world sequel.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Harvey Weinstein prison time?

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 2026 deadlock on the remaining rape charge, have reduced the likelihood of substantial additional state sentences, while his pending appeal of the 2022 California conviction for rape and sexual assault remains the primary factor keeping him incarcerated. At age 74 and with documented health issues including cancer and diabetes, traders appear to price in meaningful chances of successful appeals, time-served credits, or compassionate release that could result in little to no further effective prison time beyond what has already been served since 2020. The California sentence of 16 years is consecutive to any New York time but is actively under challenge, creating uncertainty that favors the dominant “No Prison Time” outcome in current market-implied odds. Upcoming appeal rulings or any new plea developments could shift momentum, though the pattern of hung juries and procedural setbacks has reinforced trader skepticism about lengthy additional incarceration.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: France, Spain, Argentina, England, and Portugal remain tightly grouped at the top of 2026 World Cup winner markets because each enters the group stage with elite depth, recent major-tournament pedigree, and favorable early results or rankings. France solidified its narrow lead after a convincing opening win, while Spain’s unexpected draw slightly tempered its position without derailing strong underlying form from Euro 2024 success. Argentina’s defending-champion experience and top FIFA ranking keep it competitive, and England and Portugal benefit from balanced squads and favorable paths. With 48 teams and a wide-open bracket, minor group-stage results continue to produce small odds adjustments among these contenders rather than decisive separation.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military through loyalist appointments and the absence of any promoted successor to the Politburo Standing Committee. Term limits on the presidency were eliminated in 2018, enabling his precedent-breaking third term that began in 2022, and analyses point to expectations of a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in late 2027. Recent diplomatic engagements, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and planned regional travel, reflect his active leadership without reported internal challenges or health constraints. Military purges have further centralized authority, reducing prospects for abrupt removal before the end of 2026 and aligning with trader consensus reflected in current market pricing.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the strongest position in early 2028 Democratic presidential nomination discussions, reflecting his high national profile as California governor, consistent media presence, and recent moves on issues like AI regulation that appeal to party activists. The wide field remains fragmented, with Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez drawing progressive support, Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro positioned as Senate and gubernatorial figures with records in competitive states, and Kamala Harris retaining name recognition but trailing in current trader pricing. Primary factors include post-2024 positioning debates over ideological balance, electability in battleground areas, and fundraising capacity ahead of midterms. Consolidation could occur around candidates demonstrating strong polling among key voting blocs or securing early endorsements once formal campaigns begin.
